channel.title: MT on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in MT on Detention Pipeline

item.title: 287(g) TFM: Garfield County Sheriff’s Office (MT)
item.description:
287(g) agreement between ICE and Garfield County Sheriff’s Office.

Model: TFM Signed: August 28, 2025 County: Garfield County State: MT FIPS: 30033

Source: Prison Policy Initiative appendix table, compiled from ICE data as of Feb 17, 2026.

item.title: 287(g) TFM: Montana Department of Justice (MT)

Model: TFM Signed: March 5, 2025 County: State: MT FIPS: unresolved

item.title: Big Horn County Jail, MT — Big Horn, MT
item.description:
IGSA detention facility: Big Horn County Jail, MT in Big Horn County, MT.

Source: Vera Institute ICE Detention Trends (vera-institute/ice-detention-trends)

item.title: Big Horn County, MT — USDA Distress Profile (4 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Big Horn County, MT with 4 distress indicators:

energy-transition county (mining-dependent); government/corrections-dependent economy; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio

Full flags: Mining, Government, Low Employment, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.title: Rosebud County, MT — USDA Distress Profile (3 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Rosebud County, MT with 3 distress indicators:

energy-transition county (mining-dependent); losing population; housing stress

Full flags: Mining, Housing Stress, Population Loss

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Toole County, MT with 3 distress indicators:

energy-transition county (mining-dependent); government/corrections-dependent economy; low employment ratio

Full flags: Mining, Government, Low Employment

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition
